source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

actions_taken: Fire extinguished using one 125-pound ABC wheeled fire extinguisher, two 30-pound fire extinguishers, and three firewater hoses reels
Emergency Shut Down (ESD) initiated to shut-in the facility
Alarm sounded and all work stopped
M/V Melissa directed to return to platform and placed on standby
BSEE inspectors arrived on platform to begin investigation
Platform directed not to come back online until cause determined and hazards mitigated
event.summary: On February 21, 2024, at approximately 1349 hours, a fire occurred at the HI A 379 B facility during construction operations. A construction fitter was using an electric grinder to smooth welds on deck plating when sparks migrated through two layers of protective fire blanket barriers into a chemical/methanol storage area located approximately six feet from the grinding area, igniting flammable materials. The fire was extinguished and the facility was shut down. Investigation revealed that written procedures required removal of flammable chemicals from the work area, but the construction crew elected to use fire blanket barriers as an alternative control measure, which proved inadequate.
recommendations: Ensure all approved welding and burning plans state that proper Code of Federal Regulations must be followed
Implement consistent requirements across all hot work safety documentation
Strengthen pre-job safety meetings to ensure discussion of all written control requirements
Ensure site leadership enforces compliance with Job Safety Analysis requirements

hazards.name: Flammable chemicals and hydrocarbons in proximity to hot work area
Helicopter landing operations creating swirling winds

threats.name: Sparks from grinding migrating to chemical storage area
Inadequate fire blanket barrier protection allowing spark penetration
Helicopter wind effects carrying sparks to flammable materials

consequences.name: Equipment damage (poly stock tanks, electrical cable, fire extinguishers)

controls.name: Removal of flammable chemicals and hydrocarbons from work area
controls.barrier_role: Primary control to eliminate ignition source proximity
controls.lod_basis: 30 CFR 250.113(a) and W&T Hot Work Checklist line-item 17

supporting_text: The Zealous Job Safety Analysis (JSA) clearly states 'Remove all FLAMMABLE OR COMBUSTABLE ITEMS, Make sure no Hydrocarbons are present.'
Written procedures in submitted documents clearly states to remove hydrocarbons prior to hot work commencing.

The construction crew elected to use the third control measure of the W&T Offshore Hot Work Checklist, which was erecting fire blanket barriers between the work area and the chemical storage area, versus removing all flammable or combustible items as per the Zealous JSA.

controls.name: Fire blanket barriers between work area and chemical storage
controls.barrier_role: Secondary control to shield flammable materials from sparks
controls.lod_basis: W&T Hot Work Checklist line-item 17 alternative control

supporting_text: During the grinding operations sparks migrated through two (2) layers of protective fire blanket barriers into a chemical/methanol storage area located just behind the protective barrier, approximately six feet (6') from the grinding area.
Utilizing the mentioned control measure did not eliminate the hazard.

controls.name: Firewatch monitoring during hot work operations
controls.barrier_role: Detection and response to fire initiation

supporting_text: the Firewatch notified the construction crew, and all work was stopped.
The alarm was then sounded and an Emergency Shut Down (ESD) was initiated, which shut-in the facility.

controls.name: Fire extinguishing equipment and firewater systems

supporting_text: The fire was extinguished using one (1) 125-pound (#) ABC wheeled fire extinguisher, two (2) 30 # fire extinguishers, and three (3) firewater hoses reels.

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting and Job Safety Analysis review
controls.barrier_role: Communication of hazards and control requirements

supporting_text: Interviews with both the Zealous Foreman and the W&T PIC found that no discussion or consideration was given to the two written requirements cited in the Zealous JSA and the W&T Hot Work Safety Checklist.
This is a failure in site leadership issue.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
